Scenario.overview: Communications Complex Operation Wocao, a skilled threat actor, aims to infiltrate communications networks, gather sensitive information, and cause disruption.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit, Brute-force attack, Credential compromise

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The threat actor gathers information about targets' infrastructure, personnel, and communication patterns by analyzing public sources.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 522 - Malicious Hardware Component Replacement
stage.description: The threat actor establishes command and control (C2) servers to support the operation by compromising internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2019-12991 [CWE-80]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor initiates access by sending targeted emails with malicious attachments exploiting known vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange Server.

Scenario.summary: Operation Wocao employs a complex attack strategy involving reconnaissance, resource development, initial access via spear-phishing, execution using PowerShell, and multiple post-exploitation techniques to achieve their objectives in the communications sector.
